name: Pope Leo XIV
bio: Pope Leo XIV is the current head of the Roman Catholic Church. He has identified artificial intelligence — particularly its military application and its impact on human dignity — as one of the most critical matters facing humanity. The themes are expected to anchor his first encyclical, due to be released in the coming weeks.

quotes.text: Better monitoring of how AI is being developed and used in military and civilian contexts is required, so that it does not absolve humans of responsibility for their choices and does not exacerbate the tragedy of conflicts. The combination of investments in artificial intelligence and high-tech weaponry is leading the world into a spiral of annihilation.
quotes.source: Speech at La Sapienza University, Rome — America Magazine / NPR / PBS
quotes.topic: AI in warfare; first papal visit to La Sapienza since Benedict XVI in 2008

quotes.text: Technology must serve the human person, not replace it. The proliferation of AI-generated voices and faces threatens both the dignity of those depicted and the trust on which all communication depends. Preserving human voices and faces is preserving the human person.
quotes.source: Message for the 60th World Day of Social Communications — Vatican News
quotes.topic: World Day of Social Communications; AI-generated media and human dignity

quotes.text: In this era of artificial intelligence, I encourage everyone to commit themselves to promoting forms of communication that always respect the truth of the human person, on which every technological innovation should be focused. As Laudato Si' Week begins, may all who work for an integral ecology renew their commitment.
quotes.source: Regina Caeli, Apostolic Palace window — Vatican News / Catholic World Report
quotes.topic: Pairing Magnifica Humanitas AI encyclical with Laudato Si' Week
